gloss.en: a short stay between parts of a journey

words.example: We had a stopover in Dubai.

gloss.en: a watch used to time exactly how long something takes

words.example: The coach timed us with a stopwatch.

gloss.en: to make something straight or tidy

words.example: He stood up to straighten his tie.

gloss.en: a narrow channel of sea between two areas of land

words.example: The ship sailed through the strait.

gloss.en: to kill someone by squeezing their throat

words.example: The killer tried to strangle his victim.

gloss.en: a small, narrow river

words.example: We drank water from a mountain stream.

gloss.en: to make or become more powerful or effective

words.example: Exercise can strengthen your muscles.

gloss.en: to make something longer or wider by pulling

words.example: Remember to stretch before you run.

gloss.en: that must be obeyed exactly

words.example: Our school has very strict rules.

gloss.en: in a way that must be obeyed; with firm control

words.example: She was brought up very strictly.

gloss.en: to walk with long, confident steps

words.example: He began to stride across the room.

gloss.en: when workers stop work over pay or conditions

words.example: The teachers went on strike for higher pay.

gloss.en: an act of hitting a ball with a bat or racket

words.example: What a beautiful stroke!

gloss.en: the way parts of something are arranged or organized

words.example: We studied the structure of the building.

gloss.en: a hard fight to achieve something difficult

words.example: Life was a constant struggle for them.

gloss.en: in a way that refuses to change or give up

words.example: He stubbornly refused to apologize.

gloss.en: a substance or things whose name you do not give

words.example: What's all that sticky stuff on the carpet?

gloss.en: extremely attractive or impressive

words.example: You look absolutely stunning!

gloss.en: a person who styles hair or clothes

words.example: My stylist gave me a new haircut.

gloss.en: a large part of a continent, like India

words.example: India lies on the Indian subcontinent.

gloss.en: a smaller group with its own beliefs within a society

words.example: Punk was a famous youth subculture.

gloss.en: a smaller part that something is divided into

words.example: Each chapter has a subdivision.

gloss.en: likely to be affected by something
gloss.zh: 易受影响的；可能经受的

words.example: These prices are still subject to change.

gloss.en: a verb form expressing wishes or possibility

words.example: This sentence uses the subjunctive.

gloss.en: to give a document to someone to consider

words.example: Please submit your application by Friday.

gloss.en: a secondary story within the main story

words.example: The film has a romantic subplot.

gloss.en: happening or coming after something else

words.example: The book influenced subsequent generations.

gloss.en: afterwards; later; after something else happened

words.example: The notes were subsequently lost.

gloss.en: to become less strong or intense

words.example: The pain began to subside slowly.

gloss.en: a solid, liquid or gas with particular qualities

words.example: There was a sticky substance on the floor.

gloss.en: text on screen translating what people say

words.example: I watched the film with a subtitle.

gloss.en: to take one number away from another

words.example: Subtract three from ten to get seven.

gloss.en: an area where people live outside a city centre

words.example: They live in a quiet suburb of London.

gloss.en: physical or mental pain

words.example: Death finally brought an end to her suffering.

gloss.en: enough for a particular purpose

words.example: Allow sufficient time to get there.

gloss.en: enough for a particular purpose

words.example: She felt sufficiently well to go to work.

gloss.en: in a way that is right for the situation

words.example: Please dress suitably for the interview.

gloss.en: a yellow chemical element with a strong smell

words.example: Sulfur has a strong, bad smell.

gloss.en: a yellow chemical element with a strong smell

words.example: Sulphur is used to make matches.

gloss.en: an amount of money

words.example: She paid a large sum of money.

gloss.en: a ray of sunlight

words.example: A sunbeam came through the window.

gloss.en: extremely good

words.example: We had a super meal together.

gloss.en: a person who manages or supervises a place

words.example: The superintendent runs the whole building.

gloss.en: that cannot be explained by science or nature

words.example: She believes in supernatural powers.

gloss.en: a company that provides goods or services

words.example: We found a cheaper supplier.

gloss.en: a doctor trained to perform operations

words.example: She is a famous heart surgeon.

gloss.en: medical treatment that involves cutting the body

words.example: He needs major surgery on his knee.

gloss.en: to be all around something or someone

words.example: Tall trees surround the lake.

gloss.en: that is near or around something

words.example: We explored Oxford and the surrounding area.

gloss.en: the things and places around you

words.example: The cat explored its new surroundings.

gloss.en: a study of people's opinions by asking questions

words.example: A recent survey showed most people agreed.

gloss.en: continuing to live or exist despite danger

words.example: They fought hard for survival.

gloss.en: a person who continues to live after great danger

words.example: He was the only survivor of the crash.

gloss.en: a person thought to have committed a crime

words.example: The police questioned the main suspect.

gloss.en: to hang something from something else

words.example: A lamp was suspended from the ceiling.

gloss.en: in a way that shows you do not trust something

words.example: He looked at me suspiciously.

gloss.en: using resources without harming the environment

words.example: We need sustainable energy sources.

gloss.en: to make food or drink go down your throat

words.example: Always chew food well before you swallow.

gloss.en: a large white water bird with a long neck

words.example: A white swan glided across the lake.

gloss.en: to use rude or offensive language

words.example: She fell over and began to swear loudly.

gloss.en: to clean a floor or surface using a broom

words.example: Please sweep the floor after dinner.

gloss.en: happening or moving quickly

words.example: The team made a swift decision.

gloss.en: a device you press to turn power on or off

words.example: She pressed the light switch.

gloss.en: a unit of sound in a word

words.example: The word 'water' has two syllables.

gloss.en: kind and understanding to someone's problems

words.example: She is a sympathetic listener.

gloss.en: to feel sorry for and understand someone

words.example: I really sympathise with your situation.

gloss.en: to feel sorry for and understand someone

words.example: I sympathize with people who lost their jobs.

gloss.en: the feeling of being sorry for someone

words.example: She expressed her sympathy for the family.

gloss.en: a word with the same meaning as another

words.example: 'Big' is a synonym for 'large'.

gloss.en: a cloth used to cover a table

words.example: She put a clean tablecloth on the table.

gloss.en: relating to a small popular newspaper

words.example: The story appeared in a tabloid newspaper.

gloss.en: to attach something with a small nail or pin

words.example: She tried to tack the poster to the wall.

gloss.en: connected with the sense of touch

words.example: Babies enjoy tactile toys.

gloss.en: a small label attached to identify something

words.example: He put a name tag on his bag.

gloss.en: the part at the back of an animal's body

words.example: The dog ran up, wagging its tail.

gloss.en: an imaginative story full of action

words.example: Grandpa told us a tale about pirates.

gloss.en: not wild; used to living with people

words.example: The deer was surprisingly tame.

gloss.en: brown skin colour from being in the sun

words.example: She got a deep tan on holiday.

gloss.en: a large container for liquid or gas

words.example: The car has a full fuel tank.

gloss.en: having brown skin from the sun

words.example: He looked tanned after his holiday.

gloss.en: a device that controls water flow from a pipe

words.example: She turned on the cold tap.

gloss.en: a result that you try to achieve

words.example: We hope to meet our sales target.

gloss.en: a torn and hanging piece of cloth

words.example: His old coat hung in a tatter.

gloss.en: a permanent picture or design on the skin

words.example: He has a tattoo on his arm.

gloss.en: a remark made to anger or hurt someone

words.example: She ignored the cruel taunt.

gloss.en: a hole made in something by tearing

words.example: This sheet has a tear in it.

gloss.en: a small spoon for stirring drinks

words.example: Add one teaspoon of sugar.

gloss.en: connected with practical use of machines or methods

words.example: We offer free technical support.

gloss.en: according to the exact facts or rules

words.example: Technically, she is still our manager.

gloss.en: connected with technology

words.example: There have been huge technological advances.

gloss.en: the years when you are aged 13 to 19

words.example: She began writing poetry in her teens.

gloss.en: a tube used to see distant objects clearly

words.example: We looked at the stars through a telescope.

gloss.en: having a mild climate without extremes

words.example: Britain has a temperate climate.

gloss.en: a building used for religious worship

words.example: We visited an ancient temple.

gloss.en: for a short time; not permanently

words.example: This service is temporarily unavailable.

gloss.en: lasting only a short time; not permanent

words.example: They hired some temporary workers.

gloss.en: a strong wish to do something you should not

words.example: He resisted the temptation to eat cake.

gloss.en: a likelihood of behaving in a particular way

words.example: He has a tendency to talk too much.

gloss.en: in a gentle and loving way

words.example: She held the baby tenderly.

gloss.en: the quality of being gentle and loving

words.example: He spoke with great tenderness.

gloss.en: a situation where people distrust each other

words.example: There is mounting tension along the border.

gloss.en: a word or phrase used as a name for something

words.example: 'Mouse' is a technical term in computing.

gloss.en: the conditions of an agreement or contract

words.example: Both sides agreed to the peace terms.

gloss.en: a flat outdoor area next to a building

words.example: We had dinner on the terrace.

gloss.en: very; extremely

words.example: I'm terribly sorry I'm late.

gloss.en: to make someone feel extremely frightened

words.example: Flying seems to terrify her.

gloss.en: making you feel extremely frightened

gloss.en: land controlled by a country or leader

words.example: The soldiers crossed into enemy territory.

gloss.en: a feeling of extreme fear

words.example: She screamed in pure terror.

gloss.en: using violence to achieve political aims

words.example: The bombing was an act of terrorism.

gloss.en: a person who takes part in terrorism

words.example: The terrorist was caught by police.

gloss.en: the activity of checking if something works

words.example: The country banned nuclear testing.

gloss.en: a book used to teach a school subject

words.example: Open your history textbook to page ten.

gloss.en: to such a degree; so

words.example: I can't walk that far.

gloss.en: the crime of stealing something

words.example: Car theft is a serious problem here.

gloss.en: the main subject or idea of something

words.example: Love is the main theme of the song.

gloss.en: a person who develops ideas about a subject

words.example: He is a leading political theorist.

gloss.en: a specialist who treats illness or problems

words.example: She sees a speech therapist every week.

gloss.en: treatment for a physical or mental problem

words.example: He is receiving therapy for cancer.

words.example: The nurse used a thermometer to check his fever.
